review.field: materials chemistry / solid-state physics (epitaxial multiferroic thin films)
review.note: Deterministic PASS findings (c1,c4,c13,c27) and the empty dropped_flags log are unchanged. 27 bare abstains reclassified: 21 CONFIRMED-CORRECT (6 recomputed clean: c5,c7,c9,c19,c21,c22; plus c6 numbers clean with significance routed; rest reported-value/faithful-citation with no over-reach) dropped with an auditable advisory:claims-auditor trail; 5 genuine ADVISORY concerns and 1 subjective:significance routed to human; 0 remain unassessable.
review.integrity_screen_T7: No T7 signals: numbers are internally self-consistent, no too-perfect agreement, no impossible physical values, citations faithfully represented.

routed_to_human.note: Reported RT M-H values (Mr=1.30 emu/cm3, Hc=90 Oe); abstract<->body cross-consistent and physically plausible for a weak room-temperature ferromagnet in a PFN-bearing perovskite. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote: weak ferromagnetic behavior at room temperature (remanence 1.30 emu/cm3; coercive field 90 Oe)

routed_to_human.note: Recomputed c/a = 4.096/4.022 = 1.0184, matches reported 1.018 (T0 arithmetic, exact). reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote: lattice parameters a = 4.022 ˚A, c = 4.096 ˚A, and c/a = 1.018

routed_to_human.note: Recomputed lattice mismatch (a_STO=3.905, a_film=4.022): -2.91% on a_film / -3.00% on a_STO, i.e. ~3% compressive — sign and magnitude match. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
the mismatch between the 
substrate (STO) and the film gives a value of around 3%, corresponding to compressive
strain

routed_to_human.note: Reported SRO thickness ~20 nm from XRD oscillation period; single reported figure-derived value, scope-faithful, nothing to contradict in-graph. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
The SRO layer thickness determined from the 
period of the oscillations is approximately 20 nm for both samples

routed_to_human.note: Recomputed 80 nm / 0.04 nm/pulse = 2000 pulses; thickness and growth rate are mutually self-consistent (T0). reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
The final thickness of 
the samples was around 80 nm (growth rate ≈ 0.04 nm/pulse)

routed_to_human.note: Reported C-V normalization maxima (690 pF PZT, 960 pF PZTFN0.5); exact reported values, no internal inconsistency. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote: maximum capacitance values: 690 pF for PZT and 960 pF for PZTFN0.5

routed_to_human.note: Reported PFM surface roughness ~0.82 nm; sub-nm RMS is consistent with an epitaxial PLD film and the coherent-growth claims (c30). reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
The measured surface 
roughness was approximately 0.82 nm

routed_to_human.note: Literature comparison PZTFN0.5 (1.3 emu/cm3, 90 Oe) vs PZTFT0.4 (1.58 emu/cm3, 108 Oe) [9]; 'quite similar' is faithful to the cited magnitudes. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
measured for the PZTFN0.5 epitaxial film (Mr = 1.3 emu/cm3, Hc = 90 Oe) are quite similar to 
those reported for PZTFT0.4 epitaxial films (Mr = 1.58 emu/cm3, Hc = 108 Oe) [9]

routed_to_human.note: Literature comparison Msat 12 vs 7 emu/cm3 [PZTFT0.4]; the >'higher' direction is correct against the quoted numbers. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
a magnetic saturation of 12 emu/cm3 is observed, which is higher than that for 
PZTFT0.4 films (7 emu/cm3)

routed_to_human.note: Literature comparison Mr 1.3 vs 1.8 emu/cm3 (similar) and Hc 90 vs 1500 Oe (significantly different) [14]; both qualitative characterizations match the quoted values. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
the remanences are similar (1.3 emu/cm3 and 1.8 emu/cm3 [14]), but 
there is
a significant difference in coercive field values (90 Oe versus 1500 Oe)

routed_to_human.note: Recomputed photon energy 1239.84/403 = 3.077 eV ~ 3.08 eV (T1, fixed external constant). reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
403 ± 8 nm (hν = 3.08 eV) laser source with a constant calibrated optical fluence of 
100 ± 5 mW/cm2

routed_to_human.note: Recomputed field 3 V / 80 nm = 375 kV/cm exactly (T0); back-solves to the stated 80 nm thickness. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
applied 
voltages ranging from −3 V to 3 V (±375 kV/cm)

routed_to_human.note: Band model internally consistent: D1-D2 = 1.2-0.7 = 0.5 eV equals eϕAu-eϕSRO = 5.1-4.6 = 0.5 eV; both barriers imply the same electron affinity χ = 3.9 eV, physically reasonable for a wide-gap (Eg=3.0 eV) oxide. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
D1 (PZTFN0.5/Au) with a barrier height of 1.2 eV, and D2 (SRO/PZTFN0.5) 
with a barrier height of 0.7 eV

routed_to_human.note: Model input affinities/gap (eϕSRO=4.6, eϕAu=5.1, Eg=3.0 eV) are attributed to refs [23][24][14]; values are the standard literature figures and feed the self-consistent c22 model. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
The electron affinities involved are eϕSRO = 4.6 eV [23] and eϕAu = 5.1 eV [24], considering a 
band gap Eg = 3.0 eV for PZTFN0.5 [14]

routed_to_human.note: Literature benchmark: unpoled BFO Jsc = 4.5 uA/cm2 at 405 nm/100 mW/cm2 [26]; faithfully quoted, supports the 'comparable-or-better' framing. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet

unpoled, sputter-deposited BFO films (100 nm thick) with ITO 
electrodes yielded JSC of 4.5 µA/cm2 under similar illumination conditions (λ = 405 nm, 100 
mW/cm2) [26]

routed_to_human.note: Literature benchmark: graphene/BFO/Pt Jsc up to 25 uA/cm2 [27]; faithfully quoted as an upper reference, no over-reach. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
higher values, up to 25 µA/cm2, have been reported for 
graphene/BFO/Pt heterojunctions [27]

routed_to_human.note: Reported headline result Jsc = 23 uA/cm2, Voc = -0.07 V; exact reported pair, consistent with the body and the abstract band (see c3 advisory). reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
the short-circuit photocurrent density (JSC = 23 µA/cm2) and 
opencircuit voltage (VOC = −0.07 V)

routed_to_human.note: Literature comparison PZTFT40/LSCO//MgO: Pr=13 uC/cm2, Ec=40 kV/cm [9]; faithfully quoted, contrast direction correct. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet

polarization of 13 µC/cm2 [9]. However, the coercive field is significantly lower in this case

routed_to_human.note: Literature comparison polycrystalline PZTFN0.5: Pr=10 uC/cm2, Ec=80 kV/cm [8]; faithfully quoted. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet

Polycrystalline PZTFN0.5 films deposited on Pt/Ti/SiO2/Si substrates have

routed_to_human.note: Reported SRO lattice parameters a=3.905, c=3.945 A; a matches cubic STO (coherent growth) and c>a is the expected tetragonal distortion under epitaxial constraint. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
the SRO 
conductive bottom layer coherently grows on the STO substrate with in-plane parameter a 
= 3.905 ˚A and out-of-plane parameter c = 3.945 ˚A

routed_to_human.note: Qualitative wavelength selectivity (response at 403 nm, none at 515 nm; no PZT response at 403 nm); consistent with the 3.0 eV gap of PZTFN0.5 vs 3.6 eV PZT — a coherent control result, scope-faithful. reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers it yet
routed_to_human.quote:
no response is detected under green light illumination of 515 nm. For the reference
PZT film, no significant photocurrent response is observed under 403 nm illumination

routed_to_human.note: Abstract reports Jsc 'between +/-20 uA/cm2' while the body/conclusions report 23 uA/cm2 (c26). The two differ by ~15%; the abstract value is rounded looser than (and below) the headline figure. Not an arithmetic error, but a quantitative abstract<->body inconsistency a reader could flag. What would confirm: a prose_vs_table verifier binding the abstract bound to the conclusions value, or an author clarification of which |Jsc| (peak vs representative) the +/-20 denotes.
routed_to_human.quote:
achieving Jsc values 
between ±20 µA/cm2

routed_to_human.note: Imprint field ~40 kV/cm is 'estimated from P-E loops' (a derived loop-shift quantity), but the graph carries no underlying loop offset / +Ec,-Ec pair to recompute it from. The estimate's appropriateness (how the shift was read) cannot be checked from the reported number alone. What would confirm: the +Ec and -Ec values whose midpoint gives the imprint, enabling a deterministic recompute.
routed_to_human.quote:
The estimated imprint field
for the PZTFN0.5 sample from P–E loops is approximately 40 kV/cm

routed_to_human.note: 'Saturation polarization in PZT is about 30% higher than in PZTFN0.5' is a comparative magnitude, but no absolute Psat values for either sample are extracted anywhere in the claim graph, so the 30% rests on un-reported figure reads and is not recomputable. What would confirm: the two Psat values (PZT and PZTFN0.5) so the percent difference can be recomputed deterministically.
routed_to_human.quote:
the saturation polarization reached in PZT 
is about 30% higher than in the PZTFN0.5 sample

routed_to_human.note: Retention 'clearly distinguishable up to 10^4 s' is read off a single Fig S5 decade and used as a durability bound; whether the Up/Down states truly remain separated to 10^4 s (vs extrapolated) cannot be confirmed from the plotted range. What would confirm: the measured Up/Down polarization vs time series, checked for a maintained gap at the 10^4 s point.
routed_to_human.quote:
the Up and Down polarization states remain 
clearly distinguishable up to 104 s

routed_to_human.note: 'Increase in conductivity of about one order of magnitude (ON vs OFF)' is a qualitative figure characterization; no ON-state and OFF-state current/conductivity values are extracted, so the ~10x ratio cannot be verified. What would confirm: the ON and OFF current densities at a fixed field from Fig 4a, enabling a deterministic ratio check.
routed_to_human.quote:
A notable increase in conductivity (about one order of magnitude) occurs when the light 
is switched ON compared to the OFF state

routed_to_human.note: The arithmetic is clean (film c/a 1.018 vs bulk 1.016; difference 0.002, ~0.12%), so the numbers are CONFIRMED-CORRECT. What remains is the qualitative judgment that this 'closely aligns' with bulk ceramics — whether a 0.12% tetragonality difference counts as 'close' is a domain-significance call, not a deterministic check. Routed, not scored.
routed_to_human.quote:
tetragonality closely aligns with that observed in bulk ceramics, which exhibit a c/a ratio of 
1.016 [8]
